title: Philippians 4 in 10 Minutes: Pray–Think–Do
hook: Anxiety flare-up? Try the 10-minute Pray–Think–Do from Philippians 4.
script: Anxiety flare-up? Try the 10-minute Pray–Think–Do from Philippians 4. For three minutes, tell God exactly what scares you, then thank Him for three specific gifts today. That’s Philippians 4:6. Next three minutes, redirect your thoughts: whatever is true, noble, right, pure, lovely, admirable—dwell there, and reframe the worry with, Even if this is hard, the Lord is near. That’s verses 7–8. Final three minutes, put one thing into practice: send the text, take the walk, tidy the desk, or do the next right step. Verse 9. Last minute, breathe peace in and say, God of peace, guard my heart and mind.

title: Guided Calm: A 10-Minute Anxiety Reset
hook: If you're feeling anxious, watch this 10-minute reset.
script: If you're feeling anxious, watch this 10-minute reset. Set a timer. First, pray honest and specific: Lord, I’m worried about ____; thank You for these three gifts. That opens the door to peace Philippians 4:6 promises. Next, think on what’s true and noble. Speak it out: God is near, He provides, this moment isn’t the whole story. Let that reframe the spiral. Then do one small obedient step you’ve been avoiding—reply kindly, step outside, drink water, open the Bible. Philippians 4:9 calls it practice. When the timer ends, whisper, The God of peace is with me, and feel your breath slow.

title: Stop the Spiral: Pray, Think, Do
hook: You may be overthinking this—try Pray, Think, Do in 10 minutes.
script: You may be overthinking this—try Pray, Think, Do in 10 minutes. Philippians 4 links all three for peace. Pray with gratitude turns your brain’s alarm down. Thinking on what’s true and lovely is biblical reframing. Doing one right step closes the anxiety loop. Here’s a quick run: three minutes to tell God the worry and thank Him for specifics. Three minutes to replace the worst-case with, God is near and working good. Three minutes to act on the next faithful step. One minute to breathe and repeat, The peace of God will guard my heart and mind in Christ Jesus. Save this for your next flare-up.
